articles package

Submodules

articles.admin module

Django admin configuration for the News Application.

class articles.admin.ArticleAdmin(model, admin_site)

Bases: ModelAdmin

Admin configuration for Article model.

list_display = ['title', 'author', 'publisher', 'approved', 'created_at']
list_filter = ['approved', 'created_at', 'publisher']
property media
readonly_fields = ['created_at']
save_model(request, obj, form, change)

Set author automatically if not set.

search_fields = ['title', 'content']
class articles.admin.NewsletterAdmin(model, admin_site)

Bases: ModelAdmin

Admin configuration for Newsletter model.

filter_horizontal = ['articles']
list_display = ['title', 'author', 'created_at']
list_filter = ['created_at', 'author']
property media
search_fields = ['title', 'description']
class articles.admin.PublisherAdmin(model, admin_site)

Bases: ModelAdmin

Admin configuration for Publisher model.

filter_horizontal = ['journalists', 'editors']
list_display = ['name', 'created_at']
property media
search_fields = ['name']
class articles.admin.UserAdmin(model, admin_site)

Bases: UserAdmin

Admin configuration for custom User model.

add_fieldsets = ((None, {'classes': ('wide',), 'fields': ('username', 'usable_password', 'password1', 'password2')}), ('Role Information', {'fields': ('role',)}))
fieldsets = ((None, {'fields': ('username', 'password')}), ('Personal info', {'fields': ('first_name', 'last_name', 'email')}), ('Permissions', {'fields': ('is_active', 'is_staff', 'is_superuser', 'groups', 'user_permissions')}), ('Important dates', {'fields': ('last_login', 'date_joined')}), ('Role Information', {'fields': ('role',)}), ('Subscriptions', {'fields': ('subscriptions_publishers', 'subscriptions_journalists')}))
list_display = ['username', 'email', 'role', 'is_staff', 'is_active']
list_filter = ['role', 'is_staff', 'is_active']
property media

articles.apps module

Django app configuration for articles app.

class articles.apps.ArticlesConfig(app_name, app_module)

Bases: AppConfig

default_auto_field = 'django.db.models.BigAutoField'
name = 'articles'
ready()

Import signals when app is ready. This ensures signal handlers are registered.

fields = ('username', 'role')
model

alias of User

base_fields = {'password1': <django.forms.fields.CharField object>, 'password2': <django.forms.fields.CharField object>, 'role': <django.forms.fields.TypedChoiceField object>, 'username': <django.forms.fields.CharField object>}
declared_fields = {'password1': <django.forms.fields.CharField object>, 'password2': <django.forms.fields.CharField object>}
property media

Return all media required to render the widgets on this form.

articles.models module

Models for the News Application. Defines User, Publisher, Article, and Newsletter models with role-based relationships.

class articles.models.Article(*args, **kwargs)

Bases: Model

Represents a news article written by journalists. Can optionally belong to a publisher and be approved by editors.

exception DoesNotExist

Bases: ObjectDoesNotExist

exception MultipleObjectsReturned

Bases: MultipleObjectsReturned

exception NotUpdated

Bases: ObjectNotUpdated, DatabaseError

approved

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

author

Accessor to the related object on the forward side of a many-to-one or one-to-one (via ForwardOneToOneDescriptor subclass) relation.

In the example:

class Child(Model):
    parent = ForeignKey(Parent, related_name='children')

Child.parent is a ForwardManyToOneDescriptor instance.

author_id
clean()

Validate that article belongs to either journalist OR publisher. Author must be a Journalist.

content

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

created_at

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

get_next_by_created_at(*, field=<django.db.models.fields.DateTimeField: created_at>, is_next=True, **kwargs)
get_previous_by_created_at(*, field=<django.db.models.fields.DateTimeField: created_at>, is_next=False, **kwargs)
id

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

newsletters

Accessor to the related objects manager on the forward and reverse sides of a many-to-many relation.

In the example:

class Pizza(Model):
    toppings = ManyToManyField(Topping, related_name='pizzas')

Pizza.toppings and Topping.pizzas are ManyToManyDescriptor instances.

Most of the implementation is delegated to a dynamically defined manager class built by create_forward_many_to_many_manager() defined below.

objects = <django.db.models.manager.Manager object>
publisher

Accessor to the related object on the forward side of a many-to-one or one-to-one (via ForwardOneToOneDescriptor subclass) relation.

In the example:

class Child(Model):
    parent = ForeignKey(Parent, related_name='children')

Child.parent is a ForwardManyToOneDescriptor instance.

publisher_id
title

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

Collection of articles curated by journalists or editors.

class articles.models.Publisher(*args, **kwargs)

Bases: Model

Represents a news publisher organization. Can have journalists and editors assigned.

exception DoesNotExist

Bases: ObjectDoesNotExist

exception MultipleObjectsReturned

Bases: MultipleObjectsReturned

exception NotUpdated

Bases: ObjectNotUpdated, DatabaseError

articles

Accessor to the related objects manager on the reverse side of a many-to-one relation.

In the example:

class Child(Model):
    parent = ForeignKey(Parent, related_name='children')

Parent.children is a ReverseManyToOneDescriptor instance.

Most of the implementation is delegated to a dynamically defined manager class built by create_reverse_many_to_one_manager() defined below.

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

class articles.models.User(*args, **kwargs)

Bases: AbstractUser

Custom user model supporting role-based access control. Roles: Reader, Journalist, Editor.

ROLE_CHOICES = (('Reader', 'Reader'), ('Journalist', 'Journalist'), ('Editor', 'Editor'))

class Restaurant(Model):

Place.restaurant is a ReverseOneToOneDescriptor instance.

role

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

save(*args, **kwargs)

Automatically assign user to Django Group based on role. Ensures user belongs to exactly one role group.

username

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

articles.permissions module

Custom permission classes for role-based access control. Enforces permissions based on user roles: Reader, Journalist, Editor.

class articles.permissions.IsAuthenticatedWithRole(*args: Any, **kwargs: Any)

Bases: BasePermission

Base permission that checks authentication and valid role.

has_permission(request, view)
message = 'Authentication required with valid role.'
class articles.permissions.IsEditor(*args: Any, **kwargs: Any)

Bases: BasePermission

has_object_permission(request, view, obj)
has_permission(request, view)
message = 'Only Editors can perform this action.'
class articles.permissions.IsJournalist(*args: Any, **kwargs: Any)

Bases: BasePermission

Permission class for Journalist role. Journalists can create, update, delete their own articles and newsletters.

has_object_permission(request, view, obj)
has_permission(request, view)
message = 'Journalists can only manage their own content.'
class articles.permissions.IsJournalistOrEditor(*args: Any, **kwargs: Any)

Bases: BasePermission

Permission class for Journalist or Editor roles. Journalists can manage own content, Editors can manage all content.

has_object_permission(request, view, obj)
has_permission(request, view)
message = 'Only Journalists or Editors can perform this action.'
class articles.permissions.IsReader(*args: Any, **kwargs: Any)

Bases: BasePermission

Permission class for Reader role. Readers can only view articles and newsletters.

has_object_permission(request, view, obj)
has_permission(request, view)
message = 'Readers can only view content.'
class articles.permissions.IsReaderOrJournalistOrEditor(*args: Any, **kwargs: Any)

Bases: BasePermission

Allow access to Readers, Journalists, and Editors. Readers can view only, Journalists/Editors have more permissions.

has_object_permission(request, view, obj)
has_permission(request, view)
message = 'Authentication required.'

articles.serializers module

Serializers for the News Application API. Separate serializers for Article, User, Publisher, and Newsletter models.

class articles.serializers.ArticleListSerializer(*args: Any, **kwargs: Any)

Bases: ModelSerializer

Lightweight serializer for article list views.

class Meta

Bases: object

fields = ['id', 'title', 'author_name', 'publisher_name', 'created_at']
model

alias of Article

class articles.serializers.ArticleSerializer(*args: Any, **kwargs: Any)

Bases: ModelSerializer

Serializer for Article model. Includes author and publisher details.

class Meta

Bases: object

fields = ['id', 'title', 'content', 'author', 'author_name', 'publisher', 'publisher_name', 'created_at', 'approved']
model

alias of Article

read_only_fields = ['id', 'created_at', 'approved', 'author']
create(validated_data)

Create article with author set to requesting journalist. Articles start as unapproved (approved=False).

class articles.serializers.NewsletterDetailSerializer(*args: Any, **kwargs: Any)

Bases: ModelSerializer

Detailed serializer for Newsletter with nested article data.

class Meta

Bases: object

fields = ['id', 'title', 'description', 'author', 'author_name', 'articles', 'created_at']
model

alias of Newsletter

read_only_fields = ['id', 'created_at']
class articles.serializers.NewsletterSerializer(*args: Any, **kwargs: Any)

Bases: ModelSerializer

Serializer for Newsletter model.

class Meta

Bases: object

fields = ['id', 'title', 'description', 'author', 'author_name', 'articles', 'created_at', 'article_count']
model

alias of Newsletter

read_only_fields = ['id', 'created_at']
get_article_count(obj)
class articles.serializers.PublisherSerializer(*args: Any, **kwargs: Any)

Bases: ModelSerializer

Serializer for Publisher model.

class Meta

Bases: object

fields = ['id', 'name', 'journalists', 'editors', 'created_at', 'journalist_count']
model

alias of Publisher

read_only_fields = ['id', 'created_at']
get_journalist_count(obj)
class articles.serializers.UserSerializer(*args: Any, **kwargs: Any)

Bases: ModelSerializer

Serializer for User model. Handles role-based field exposure.

class Meta

Bases: object

fields = ['id', 'username', 'email', 'role', 'subscriptions_publishers', 'subscriptions_journalists', 'date_joined', 'last_login']
model

alias of User

read_only_fields = ['id', 'date_joined', 'last_login']
create(validated_data)

Create user with password hashing and role-based group assignment.

articles.signals module

Django signals for the News Application. Handles article approval notifications.

articles.signals.article_approved_signal(sender, instance, created, **kwargs)
articles.signals.notify_internal_api(article)

Send POST request to internal API endpoint when article is approved. Endpoint: /api/approved/

articles.signals.send_approval_notifications(article)

Send email notifications to subscribers when article is approved. Subscribers include: - Readers subscribed to the author (journalist) - Readers subscribed to the publisher (if article has publisher)

articles.signals.store_old_approved(sender, instance, **kwargs)

articles.tests module

Test suite for the News Application. Tests role-based access, subscriptions, article approval, and signal behavior.

class articles.tests.ArticleApprovalTest(*args: Any, **kwargs: Any)

Bases: TestCase

Test article approval workflow.

setUp()
test_approved_articles_visible()

Test that approved articles are visible to readers.

test_article_created_unapproved()

Test that articles are created as unapproved.

test_editor_can_approve()

Test that editor can approve an article.

class articles.tests.ArticleModelTest(*args: Any, **kwargs: Any)

Bases: TestCase

Test cases for the Article model.

setUp()
test_article_author_must_be_journalist()

Test that article author must be a journalist.

test_article_creation()

Test creating an article.

test_article_with_publisher()

Test creating an article with a publisher.

class articles.tests.NewsletterModelTest(*args: Any, **kwargs: Any)

Bases: TestCase

Test cases for the Newsletter model.

setUp()
test_newsletter_articles()

Test adding articles to newsletter.

test_newsletter_creation()

Test creating a newsletter.

class articles.tests.NewsletterPermissionTest(*args: Any, **kwargs: Any)

Bases: TestCase

Test newsletter permissions.

setUp()
test_editor_can_update_any_newsletter()

Test that editors can update any newsletter.

test_journalist_can_create_newsletter()

Test that journalists can create newsletters.

test_journalist_cannot_update_others_newsletter()

Test that journalists cannot update others’ newsletters.

test_reader_cannot_create_newsletter()

Test that readers cannot create newsletters.

class articles.tests.PermissionDeniedTest(*args: Any, **kwargs: Any)

Bases: TestCase

Test permission denied scenarios.

setUp()
test_editor_can_delete_any_article()

Test that editors can delete any article.

test_editor_can_update_any_article()

Test that editors can update any article.

test_journalist_cannot_delete_others_article()

Test that journalists cannot delete other journalists’ articles.

test_journalist_cannot_update_others_article()

Test that journalists cannot update other journalists’ articles.

test_reader_cannot_update_article()

Test that readers cannot update articles.

class articles.tests.PublisherModelTest(*args: Any, **kwargs: Any)

Bases: TestCase

Test cases for the Publisher model.

test_publisher_creation()

Test creating a publisher.

test_publisher_unique_name()

Test that publisher names are unique.

class articles.tests.RoleBasedAccessTest(*args: Any, **kwargs: Any)

Bases: TestCase

Test role-based access control.

setUp()
test_editor_can_approve_article()

Test that editors can approve articles.

test_journalist_can_create_article()

Test that journalists can create articles.

test_reader_can_view_articles()

Test that readers can view approved articles.

test_reader_cannot_create_article()

Test that readers cannot create articles.

class articles.tests.SignalBehaviorTest(*args: Any, **kwargs: Any)

Bases: TestCase

Test Django signal behavior on article approval.

setUp()
test_signal_not_triggered_on_creation()

Test that signal is not triggered when article is created.

test_signal_sends_api_request_on_approval(mock_post)

Test that signal sends POST request to API on approval.

test_signal_sends_email_on_approval(mock_post)

Test that signal sends email when article is approved.

class articles.tests.SubscriptionFilteringTest(*args: Any, **kwargs: Any)

Bases: TestCase

Test subscription-based article filtering.

setUp()
test_reader_does_not_see_unsubscribed_articles()

Test that reader doesn’t see articles from unsubscribed sources.

test_reader_sees_subscribed_articles()

Test that reader sees articles from subscribed sources.

class articles.tests.UserModelTest(*args: Any, **kwargs: Any)

Bases: TestCase

Test cases for the custom User model.

test_user_auto_group_assignment()

Test that user is automatically assigned to group based on role.

test_user_creation()

Test creating a user with default role.

test_user_with_editor_role()

Test creating an editor user.

test_user_with_journalist_role()

Test creating a journalist user.

articles.views module

Views for the News Application. Includes API views and traditional Django views for web interface.

articles.views.api_docs_view(request)

Displays API documentation page.

articles.views.approve_article_view(request, pk)

Allows Editors to approve articles.

articles.views.article_detail_view(request, pk)

Displays a single article based on user role permissions.

articles.views.article_list_view(request)

Displays list of articles filtered by user role and subscriptions.

articles.views.create_article_view(request)

Allows Journalists to create new articles.

articles.views.create_newsletter_view(request)

Allows Journalists and Editors to create newsletters.

articles.views.create_publisher_view(request)

Allows Editors to create new publishers.

articles.views.delete_article_view(request, pk)

Allows Journalists or Editors to delete articles based on permissions.

articles.views.edit_article_view(request, pk)

Allows Journalists or Editors to edit articles depending on permissions.

articles.views.edit_newsletter_view(request, pk)

Allows editing of newsletters for Journalists and Editors.

articles.views.edit_publisher_view(request, pk)

Allows Editors to edit publisher details.

articles.views.editor_dashboard_view(request)

Dashboard for Editors to manage all articles.

articles.views.journalist_dashboard_view(request)

Dashboard showing Journalist’s own articles and newsletters.

articles.views.landing_page(request)

Displays landing page with featured approved articles.

articles.views.newsletter_detail_view(request, pk)

Displays a single newsletter with its articles.

articles.views.newsletter_list_view(request)

Displays all newsletters.

articles.views.publisher_detail_view(request, pk)

Shows details of a publisher and its approved articles.

articles.views.publisher_list_view(request)

Displays list of all publishers.

articles.views.signup(request)

Handles user registration using CustomUserCreationForm.

articles.views.subscribe_journalist_view(request, pk)

Toggles subscription to a journalist for Readers.

articles.views.subscribe_publisher_view(request, pk)

Toggles subscription to a publisher for Readers.

Module contents

Built with Sphinx using a theme provided by Read the Docs.
